questions.text: It was once proposed to use a 2 km vertical Sudbury mine shaft for microgravity experiments in a vacuum. How much time would scientists have to do an experiment during one drop?
If the moon were twice as massive as it is now, and it stayed at the same orbital radius about the earth as it has now, its new orbital period (in terms of its current orbital period T) would be,
In the following circuit composed of identical capacitors, across which terminals would you connect a battery in order for all the capacitors to charge up.
questions.diagram_description: Circuit diagram showing capacitors arranged in a network with terminals labeled A, B, C, and D

questions.text: Two cylindrical resistors, one of length l and radius r, and the other of length 3l and radius 3r, are made of the same materials. If the resistance of the smaller one is R, what is the resistance of the larger one?
A simple pendulum consists of a mass m attached to a light string of length l. If the system is oscillating through small angles, which of the following is true?

An astronaut in the space shuttle orbiting the earth performs a trick for a television audience. She inflates a helium filled balloon within the shuttle's controlled atmosphere and lets go of it. To the astonishment of all watching, the balloon

choices.a: hovers in place where it was released.
choices.b: rises noticeably away from the earth.
choices.d: drifts backwards opposite to the direction of the shuttle's velocity.

questions.text: A boat has a green light (with wavelength λ = 500 nm) on its mast. What wavelength would be measured and what colour would be observed for this light as seen by a diver submerged in water (index of refraction n = 1.33) by the side of the boat.
A radio station transmits using a vertical mast. The antenna in your radio is a coil (solenoid) wrapped around a ferrite (iron) rod. What orientation must this antenna have for optimum reception?

choices.c: Horizontal rod pointing 90° from the line of the mast.
choices.d: Horizontally oriented rod directly above the mast.

questions.text: You have ten identical filters. One is placed in front of a white light and the light appears red. When all ten are placed in front of the light, it appears to be a dim green colour. Which of the following is a possible transmission characteristic for one of the filters?
questions.diagram_description: Four graphs (a, b, c, d) showing transmission T vs wavelength with regions marked G (green), R (red), and λ. Each graph shows different transmission patterns across the spectrum.

questions.text: Two atoms interact with each other according to the following force F, and potential, V diagrams. What is their equilibrium separation?

choices.a: The separation u which is equal to v
choices.b: The separation u which is equal to z
choices.c: The separation w which is equal to y
choices.d: The separation w which is equal to z

questions.diagram_description: Two graphs showing force F vs distance x and potential V vs distance x, with labeled points u, v, w on the force graph and y, z on the potential graph.

questions.text: A ball is thrown into the air with an initial speed u. The time interval taken for the ball to rise to its maximum

An airplane flies a straight path from town A to town B, 500 km away. Town B is due east of town A and a strong wind blows from north to south at 300 km/hr. If the plane's airspeed is 900 km/hr, which of the following statements is true?

choices.c: Plane's heading is 30° North of East.

questions.text: Microwaves of wavelength λ = 5.0 cm, and intensity $I_0$, are split and recombined by the metallic mirror system shown. What should be so that the intensity of the microwaves at the point P (the detector) is zero.
questions.diagram_description: Shows a microwave interferometer with two paths - one direct and one with mirrors creating a path difference x. Point P is the detector location.

questions.text: Which of the following is a possible expression for the Rydberg, a unit of energy?
Consider the network of identical resistors shown. The equivalent resistance between the points A and B is,
questions.diagram_description: Shows a rectangular network of resistors with points A and B. The network contains multiple resistors of value R arranged in a grid pattern between points A and B.

questions.text: A ray of light is directed towards a corner reflector as shown. The incident ray makes an angle of 22° with one of the mirrors. At what angle θ does the ray emerge?
questions.diagram_description: Shows a corner reflector (two mirrors at 90° to each other) with an incident ray making a 22° angle with one mirror, and an emerging ray at angle θ.

questions.text: Many people's glasses appear to be a blue-green colour when viewed under reflected light. A thin film of index of refraction n = 1.35 is applied to the outside surface of the glass so that the film/glass interface does not reflect any red light of wavelength λ = 630 nm. What thickness must the film layer be in order to achieve this? Take the index of refractions of air and glass to be 1.0 and 1.6 respectively.
A mass M has the same kinetic energy as a mass m. The ratio of their momenta, $p_M/p_m$, is,

A stream of water droplets, each of mass m = 0.001 kg, are fired horizontally at a velocity of 10 m/s towards a steel plate where they collide. The droplets are spaced equidistantly with a spacing of 1 cm. What is the approximate average force exerted on the plate by the water droplets assuming that they do not rebound after their collision.

Three identical balls are thrown from the top of a cliff and some time later land at the base of the cliff. Ball A is thrown upwards with speed v, Ball B is thrown downwards with speed v, and Ball C is thrown at speed v and at an angle of 45° above the horizontal. Comparing the speeds, $v_A$, $v_B$, and $v_C$ with which the balls hit the ground at the base of the cliff (and ignoring air resistance), you find,
